pattern: Only the parity of the path length matters: among d edges exactly half of the two-to-the-d weightings give an odd sum, so each query reduces to a power of two over its tree distance.
hints: An edge weight of 1 is odd and 2 is even, so the path sum's parity is just how many edges got weight 1.
Among d edges, exactly half of all assignments produce an odd count of weight-ones, giving 2^(d-1) ways when d is at least 1, and 0 when the path is empty.
Precompute depths and an ancestor table so each query's path length is depth[u]+depth[v]-2*depth[lca]; answer 2^(len-1) modulo 1e9+7, or 0 for length zero.

edge_inputs.description: single edge tree with self and adjacent queries
branching tree with paths of length two and three
every query is a self-loop of length zero
path that crosses the root via the lca

stress.description: random tree by attaching each new node to an existing one, mixed queries
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(2, size)
    edges = []
    for v in range(2, n + 1):
        u = rng.randint(1, v - 1)
        edges.append([u, v])
    q = rng.randint(1, 200)
    queries = [[rng.randint(1, n), rng.randint(1, n)] for _ in range(q)]
    return [edges, queries]
stress.note: valid tree by construction; includes self-loops by chance
